citations.narrative:
During Institutional Animal Care & Use Committee records review, a significant deficiency was documented that all
animals had not been fed on 5 July 2025. While the larger animals have freely available food, the rabbit portions are
measured daily and they went without food for that day. Rabbits shall be fed at least once each day, of sufficient quantity
and nutritive value to meet the normal daily requirements for the condition and size of the rabbit.
Corrected before the date of inspection on 16 March 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with IACUC representatives.
n

narrative:
3.54(a) 
   
Feeding. 
During Institutional Animal Care & Use Committee records review, a significant deficiency was documented that all 
animals had not been fed on 5 July 2025. While the larger animals have freely available food, the rabbit portions are 
measured daily and they went without food for that day. Rabbits shall be fed at least once each day, of sufficient quantity 
and nutritive value to meet the normal daily requirements for the condition and size of the rabbit.  
 
Corrected before the date of inspection on 16 March 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with IACUC representatives. 
 
 n
